record.field:
# Sync customer invoices (SO → Delivery → Invoice → Payment) for previous day # datetime and timedelta are already available in context
# Get yesterday's date yesterday = (datetime.datetime.now() - datetime.timedelta(days=1)).date()
# Get the first mssql.sync record (or create if doesn't exist) sync_record = model.search([], limit=1) if not sync_record: raise UserError('No MSSQL Sync configuration found. Please create one first.')
# Set the invoice_date field to yesterday and run CI sync log("Running daily CI sync (action_create_invoice) for %s" % yesterday) sync_record.write({'invoice_date': yesterday}) sync_record.action_create_invoice() log("Daily CI sync completed for %s" % yesterday)
MSSQL: Daily Master Data Sync (New Records Only)

# Sync master data (products, vendors, customers) daily # ONLY ADDS NEW RECORDS - does not update existing records # Skips existing records to avoid overwriting manual changes in Odoo # datetime and timedelta are already available in context
# Get the first mssql.sync record sync_record = model.search([], limit=1) if not sync_record: raise UserError('No MSSQL Sync configuration found. Please create one first.')
# Sync products (only new ones) log("Running daily master data sync - Products (new records only)") try: sync_record.sync_products() log("✅ Products sync completed") except Exception as e: log("❌ Products sync failed: %s" % str(e))
# Sync vendors (only new ones) log("Running daily master data sync - Vendors (new records only)") try: sync_record.sync_vendors() log("✅ Vendors sync completed") except Exception as e: log("❌ Vendors sync failed: %s" % str(e))
# Sync customers (only new ones) log("Running daily master data sync - Customers (new records only)") try: sync_record.sync_customers() log("✅ Customers sync completed") except Exception as e: log("❌ Customers sync failed: %s" % str(e))
log("Daily master data sync completed")
